etymology.description: 品(品) (pǐn, "three mouths") + 木(木) (mù, "tree") = chirping of birds in a tree -> original form of 噪(噪) (zào, "noisy")
etymology.interpretation: 喿(喿) (zào, "used phonetically") combines the concept of multiple mouths with a tree to depict the chirping sounds of birds, emphasizing the idea of collective noise. It later became the original form of 噪(噪) (zào, "noisy") which directly relates to noise made by mouths.
